region_of_origin: Southeast Asia (widely used in Singapore, Malaysia, Indonesia) / East Asia (Korea)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general anchovy food product class; no specific 'dried whole anchovy' subclass exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for dried anchovy as a food product; not fabricated.

serving.description: 1 tablespoon (approx. 10g) as flavouring or small snack portion
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of dried anchovy as a flavouring base or condiment is approximately 10g (about 1 tablespoon), yielding roughly 22.5 kcal, 5g protein, and 352mg sodium.

nutrient_source.title: Anchovy, European, dried — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 175159); cross-referenced with Singapore HPB Foodo database entry for Dried anchovy, whole (cr_id F03040027)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) FoodData Central; Health Promotion Board (HPB), Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, dietary fibre, calcium, iron) sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for dried anchovy, whole (cr_id F03040027, confidence 0.95). Sodium (3520mg/100g), saturated fat (0.6g/100g), and potassium (1200mg/100g) adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for dried anchovy (FDC ID 175159), as HPB does not report these values for this entry. Sugar confirmed as 0g per HPB source. USDA SR Legacy is an approved secondary global source per Project Veritas v2 source policy.

gi_source.title: N/A — Dried anchovy, whole contains negligible carbohydrates (0.5g/100g); glycemic index is not applicable
gi_source.source_match_notes: Dried anchovy, whole contains only 0.5g carbohydrate per 100g. Per Project Veritas v2 protocol for low/zero-carbohydrate foods (pure proteins, fish and seafood with negligible carbohydrates), glycemic_index is set to null (not 0), gi_category is set to 'not_applicable', and glycemic_load_per_serving is set to 0.0. No GI value is assigned or estimated. This food type is explicitly listed in the Veritas critical rule for negligible-carbohydrate foods.

hypertension.key_factor: Extremely high sodium content (~3520mg/100g) from salting and drying process
hypertension.guidance: Dried anchovies should be used only as a flavouring base in very small quantities (5–10g per dish) for hypertensive individuals; rinsing before use and discarding the anchovies after stock-making can substantially reduce sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate (0.5g/100g) and very high protein (50.1g/100g) support blood glucose stability
type2_diabetes.guidance: Dried anchovy is an excellent low-carbohydrate, high-protein flavouring for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; avoid sweet-glazed or sugar-seasoned anchovy preparations that add significant carbohydrate load, and monitor sodium intake carefully.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium (3520mg/100g) is a cardiovascular risk factor, partially offset by beneficial omega-3 fatty acids (EPA/DHA) and very low saturated fat (0.6g/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For cardiovascular disease patients, dried anchovy should be limited to small flavouring quantities (5–10g) due to its very high sodium content, while its omega-3 fatty acid content and low saturated fat profile are beneficial when consumed in moderation.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Rich in omega-3 fatty acids (EPA/DHA) and calcium (905mg/100g), beneficial for joint inflammation and bone protection, but very high sodium worsens tissue swelling
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Use dried anchovy only in small quantities as a flavouring for RA patients; rinse before use to reduce sodium, and avoid deep-fried anchovy preparations as AGEs generated by frying worsen joint inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Dried anchovy (whole) is a traditional processed seafood product made by salting and sun-drying small anchovies (Engraulis spp.) until they reach a firm, crispy texture with a silvery-grey to brown colour. The drying and salting process concentrates nutrients dramatically, resulting in an exceptionally high protein content of 50.1g per 100g, very high calcium at 905mg per 100g, and significant iron at 4mg per 100g, making it one of the most nutrient-dense small fish products available. The fat content is very low at 2.5g per 100g with minimal saturated fat (~0.6g), and carbohydrate is negligible at 0.5g per 100g. The dominant clinical concern is the extremely high sodium content of approximately 3520mg per 100g, a direct consequence of the salting process used in preservation. Dried anchovies are a staple ingredient across Southeast Asian cuisines — particularly in Singapore, Malaysia, and Indonesia — where they are used as a flavouring base for stocks (e.g., anchovy broth for laksa or noodle soups), stir-fried as a crispy side dish (ikan bilis goreng), or served alongside nasi lemak. In Korean cuisine, a closely related product (myeolchi) is used extensively for making dashima-myeolchi broth. The food is suitable for ketogenic and high-protein dietary patterns. Clinically, it is appropriate for Type 2 Diabetes management due to its negligible carbohydrate content, but must be used with caution in hypertension and cardiovascular disease due to its very high sodium load. Its omega-3 fatty acid content (EPA/DHA) provides anti-inflammatory benefits relevant to cardiovascular and rheumatoid arthritis management. Glycemic index is not applicable given the negligible carbohydrate content.

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F03040027, confidence 0.95), an approved primary regional source. Sodium, saturated fat, and potassium cross-referenced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 175159), an approved secondary global source. Source match is exact for the primary HPB entry, with documented adaptation for missing nutrients from USDA SR Legacy.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for dried whole anchovy not reported in HPB Singapore Foodo database; USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 175159) reports total fat but not separated MUFA/PUFA for this specific dried preparation. cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrient values are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this food item, and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 175159) does not provide a complete micronutrient panel for this specific dried whole anchovy preparation. trans_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero — dried anchovy is a natural fish product with no industrial trans fats. sugar_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo source. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: confirmed zero — fish product contains no dietary fibre. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0.5g/100g); glycemic_index set to null per Veritas protocol for low/zero-carbohydrate foods including all fish and seafood with negligible carbohydrates. Sodium value of 3520mg/100g is adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 175159, dried salted anchovy); the HPB source does not report sodium directly for this entry. Potassium value of 1200mg/100g is adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy anchovy data. Saturated fat of 0.6g/100g is adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy anchovy data as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown.
